title: Object-Oriented Programming Lab
description: Laboratory component for CSC 144. Hands-on practice with object-oriented programming concepts.

title: Object-Oriented Programming
description: Introduction to object-oriented programming principles. Covers classes, inheritance, polymorphism, and encapsulation.

title: Data Structures
description: Study of fundamental data structures including linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs, with algorithm analysis.

title: Software Architecture and Patterns
description: Study of software design patterns and architectural principles for building scalable, maintainable systems.

title: Database and Information Processing Systems
description: Covers relational database design, SQL, and information processing systems. NOTE: Most upper-level Math and CS courses offered in alternate years.

title: Intro to AI/ML Programming
description: Introduction to artificial intelligence and machine learning programming concepts and applications.

title: Intro to Distributed and Cloud Computing
description: Fundamentals of distributed systems and cloud computing architectures and services.

title: Programming Languages
description: Survey of programming language paradigms including functional, logical, and imperative languages.

title: Cryptography
description: Mathematical foundations of cryptography including symmetric and asymmetric encryption, hash functions, and protocols.

title: Junior Seminar
description: Junior-level seminar for CS and Math students. Professional development and academic discourse.

title: Algorithms and Complexity
description: Design and analysis of algorithms, computational complexity theory, and problem-solving strategies.

title: Web Dev: The Front End
description: Modern front-end web development including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and contemporary frameworks.

title: Web Dev: The Back End
description: Server-side web development including APIs, databases, authentication, and deployment.

title: Computer Architecture and Operating Systems
description: Study of computer hardware architecture and operating system design including processes, memory management, and I/O. Also offered online for Austria semester.

title: Senior Capstone Project I
description: First semester of the senior capstone. Students define and begin a substantial software project.

title: Senior Capstone Project II
description: Continuation and completion of the senior capstone project with final presentation.

title: Introduction to Software Engineering
description: Overview of the software engineering discipline, its processes, tools, and professional practices.

title: Linux and Scripting
description: Introduction to Linux operating system and shell scripting for software development and system administration.

title: Requirements Analysis
description: Techniques for gathering, analyzing, and documenting software requirements. Offered every other year — check with advisor for sequencing.

title: Intro to Cybersecurity and Networking
description: Fundamentals of networking protocols and cybersecurity principles including threats, defenses, and best practices.

title: Virtue, Technology, and Society
description: Examination of the ethical and moral dimensions of technology within a Catholic intellectual framework.

title: Mathematical Foundations of AI and ML
description: Mathematical foundations underpinning artificial intelligence and machine learning algorithms.

title: Cybersecurity I: Computer Security
description: Principles of computer security including authentication, access control, and vulnerability assessment.

title: Cybersecurity II: Network Security and Management
description: Advanced network security concepts including intrusion detection, firewalls, and security management.

title: Cybersecurity III: Software and Security
description: Secure software development practices, code auditing, and application security.

title: Software Process and Quality Assurance
description: Software development lifecycles, process models, quality assurance, and testing methodologies.

title: Software Process and Quality Assurance Lab
description: Laboratory component for SFE 384. Hands-on application of software quality assurance techniques.

title: Senior Capstone Project I
description: First semester of the senior capstone for SFE majors.

title: Senior Capstone Project II
description: Completion of the senior capstone project for SFE majors.

title: Computer Architecture and Operating Systems
description: Study of computer hardware architecture and operating systems for SFE students. Also offered online for Austria semester.

title: Advanced Cybersecurity Topics
description: Advanced topics in cybersecurity including forensics, penetration testing, and emerging threats.

title: Introduction to Engineering Analysis
description: Introduction to engineering problem-solving and analysis methodologies.

title: Engineering Analysis
description: Applied engineering analysis including numerical methods and computational tools.

title: Introduction to Electrical Systems Engineering
description: Fundamentals of electrical circuits and systems for software engineers.

title: Analytic Geometry and Calculus I
description: Introduction to differential calculus including limits, derivatives, and applications.

title: Analytic Geometry and Calculus II
description: Integral calculus, sequences, series, and applications.

title: Matrix Theory I
description: Introduction to matrices, vectors, and linear systems.

title: Matrix Theory II
description: Continuation of matrix theory including eigenvalues and transformations.

title: Probability and Statistics
description: Introductory probability and statistics for science and engineering students.

title: Discrete Mathematics
description: Logic, sets, relations, functions, combinatorics, and graph theory as applied to computing.

title: Graph Theory
description: Study of graphs and their applications in computer science and combinatorics.

title: Intermediate Linear Algebra
description: Vector spaces, linear transformations, eigenvalues, and applications.

title: Mathematical Statistics
description: Advanced probability theory and mathematical statistics.

title: University Physics I
description: Calculus-based mechanics, kinematics, dynamics, energy, and momentum.

title: University Physics I Lab
description: Laboratory component for PHY 220.

title: University Physics III
description: Electricity, magnetism, circuits, and electromagnetic waves.

title: University Physics III Lab
description: Laboratory component for PHY 224.

title: Foundations of Ethics
description: Introduction to ethical theory in the Catholic philosophical tradition.

title: Catholicism, Science, and Technology I
description: First in a series exploring the relationship between Catholic faith and scientific/technological inquiry.

title: Catholicism, Science, and Technology II
description: Second in the series on Catholicism, science, and technology.

title: Catholicism, Science, and Technology III
description: Third in the series on Catholicism, science, and technology.

title: Catholicism, Science, and Technology IV
description: Final course in the Catholicism, Science, and Technology series.

title: Introduction to Statistics (Economics)
description: Statistical methods applied to economic and social data.
